ip_outcome_note: Card name explicitly identifies the figure as Salza, leader of Cooler's Armored Squadron. Union Force is an official Bandai-licensed DBS TCG product. Salza is a canonical character from Dragon Ball Z: Cooler's Revenge (Movie 5, 1991) — internal Toei Animation IP. No external crossover risk. This card also closes the wave-65 Angila misattribution: Angila (BT2-or-adjacent) is from Dragon Ball Z: Lord Slug (Movie 4, 1991), a different film with a different villain, not from Cooler's Revenge. The Armored Squadron is Salza, Dore, and Neiz — NOT Angila, who serves Lord Slug.

trivia_bullets.text: IP verified: Card name directly identifies Salza as the leader of Cooler's Armored Squadron. Salza is the captain of the three-member mercenary platoon (Salza, Dore, and Neiz) that works under Cooler, Frieza's brother, in Dragon Ball Z: Cooler's Revenge — the fifth Dragon Ball Z theatrical film, released in Japan on July 20, 1991 [WebSearch: Salza Cooler's Armored Squadron Dragon Ball Z Cooler's Revenge 1991; Wikipedia: Dragon Ball Z: Cooler's Revenge]. Salza's name is a pun on the Spanish sauce 'salsa' — consistent with the condiment-naming pattern of Cooler's squad [WebSearch: Dragon Ball Wiki via search snippet].
sources: WebSearch: Salza Cooler's Armored Squadron Dragon Ball Z Cooler's Revenge film 1991 Angila Neiz

trivia_bullets.text: Wave-65 correction: Angila (the villain previously misattributed to this slot) is from Dragon Ball Z: Lord Slug — Movie 4, a separate 1991 film featuring Lord Slug, a Namekian villain, and his mercenary trio. Angila is Lord Slug's right-hand man: tall with cyan skin, pointy ears, short horns, and blonde hair in a toga [WebSearch: Angila Dragon Ball Z Lord Slug villain]. Salza (this card) is from Movie 5 under Cooler — an entirely different film, villain, and mercenary group. The confusion appears to stem from both films releasing in the same year (1991) with both featuring a named three-person villain squad.
sources: WebSearch: Angila Dragon Ball Z Lord Slug film villain mercenary NOT Cooler
WebSearch: Salza Cooler's Armored Squadron Dragon Ball Z Cooler's Revenge film 1991

trivia_bullets.text: Salza originates from planet Coola no. 98 — the same star system as Ginyu Force member Jeice — establishing a background lore link between Cooler's Armored Squadron and Frieza's Ginyu Force as parallel elite mercenary units serving the Cold family's space empire [WebSearch: Dragon Ball Wiki Cooler's Armored Squadron via search snippet]. This planet-of-origin detail positions Salza as an elite within a known imperial framework rather than a random mercenary.
sources: WebSearch: Salza Cooler's Armored Squadron Dragon Ball Z Cooler's Revenge film 1991 Angila Neiz

trivia_bullets.text: Union Force (DBS-B02) released November 2017 by Bandai [WebSearch: Dragon Ball Super Union Force BT2 2017]. As an uncommon card featuring a secondary film antagonist (Salza appears only in Cooler's Revenge and its sequel), this card targets the collector audience invested in the DBZ movie continuity rather than the main series canon.
sources: WebSearch: Dragon Ball Super Union Force BT2 release date 2017 Bandai

related_cards.name: Dore, Cooler's Armored Squadron
